reason: The statement applies because in the text fragment, Saul is commanded by the LORD to utterly destroy the Amalekites, sparing nothing, and is warned of the consequences of disobedience. Saul disobeys by sparing Agag and the best of the livestock, leading to his rejection as king by the LORD, demonstrating the threat of great destruction to a city (Amalek) due to Saul's actions.
